chunks:
# ==============================================================================
# Chapter 2: Intent-Verified Development: A New Paradigm
# ==============================================================================
# Module-level intent for the second chapter of the IVD book. Introduces IVD
# as the solution to the alignment crisis established in Chapter 1.
#
# Reference: book/book_system_intent.yaml (system)
# Parent:    book_structure.part_1_foundations.chapters[1]
# Version: 1.3
# Created: 2026-01-26
# Status: Complete
# ==============================================================================

parent_intent: "../../../book_system_intent.yaml"

scope:
  level: "module"
  type: "chapter"
  granularity: "coarse"

# ==============================================================================
# INTENT: What should this chapter accomplish?
# ==============================================================================

intent:
  summary: "Introduce Intent-Verified Development as the framework for the AI Agents era. Present the key insight: the AI writes the intent, implements against it, and verifies. Overview of the eight principles."
  
  goal: |
    By the end of this chapter, the reader:
    1. Understands IVD is the framework for the AI Agents era (not traditional dev)
    2. Grasps the key insight: the AI writes the intent artifact (not the human), then implements and verifies
    3. Sees IVD in action (CSV example): how many-turns drops to one, how hallucinations are caught
    4. Knows the eight principles by name and one-line summary (deep dive in Ch3)
    5. Believes that "executable understanding fails loudly" catches hallucinations at the source
    
    This chapter INTRODUCES IVD as the solution to many-turns and hallucinations. Chapter 3 goes deep on the principles.
  
  success_metric: |
    - Reader can state "Intent is Primary" and explain that the AI writes the intent
    - Reader understands why IVD solves many-turns and hallucinations
    - Reader sees how the CSV scenario changes: AI writes intent, implements, verifies
    - Reader can name at least 3 of the 8 principles
    - Chapter ends with reader wanting to learn the principles in depth (Ch3 hook)
  
  stakeholders:
    - "IVD book author (manuscript)"
    - "book/book_system_intent.yaml (part_1 purpose: establish the paradigm)"

# ==============================================================================
# DELIVERABLES: Required sections
# ==============================================================================

deliverables:
  sections:
    - id: "2.1"
      title: "The Three Paradigms: Traditional → Literate → Intent-Verified"
      required_content:
        - "Traditional: Human developer reads requirements, interprets, builds. AI agents broke this model."
        - "Literate programming: Designed for humans, not AI. AI cannot verify prose."
        - "Intent-Verified: The AI writes, reads, and verifies structured intent. The paradigm for AI agents."
        - "Table: Can the AI verify it? (Traditional: No, Literate: No, IVD: Yes)"
    
    - id: "2.2"
      title: "The Core Insight: Intent is Primary"
      required_content:
        - "Inten

erables:
  sections:
    - id: "2.1"
      title: "The Three Paradigms: Traditional → Literate → Intent-Verified"
      required_content:
        - "Traditional: Human developer reads requirements, interprets, builds. AI agents broke this model."
        - "Literate programming: Designed for humans, not AI. AI cannot verify prose."
        - "Intent-Verified: The AI writes, reads, and verifies structured intent. The paradigm for AI agents."
        - "Table: Can the AI verify it? (Traditional: No, Literate: No, IVD: Yes)"
    
    - id: "2.2"
      title: "The Core Insight: Intent is Primary"
      required_content:
        - "Intent describes WHAT and WHY; code describes HOW; docs explain the code"
        - "Intent survives implementation: rewriting Python → Go doesn't change what the system should do"
        - "Intent is technology-agnostic: says 'qualify leads', not 'use XGBoost'"
        - "Quotable: **Intent is Primary. Code and documentation are derived.**"
    
    - id: "2.3"
      title: "The Contract Model: The AI Writes and Verifies"
      required_content:
        - "Key insight: The AI writes the intent artifact (not the human), following IVD framework"
        - "Workflow: You describe → AI writes intent → You review → AI implements → AI verifies"
        - "Table: Who writes it? (PRD: Human, Prompt: Human, Intent Artifact: AI)"
        - "Why this reduces turns: clarification at intent stage, not after code exists"
        - "Linguistic mirroring as early alignment signal (AI echoing key terms = understanding)"
        - "When you don't understand the concepts: teaching step (AI creates educational artifact, you confirm understanding, then proceed); part of Principle 6 (Step 0a)"
        - "When you're not sure what to ask: discovery step (AI proposes options, you pick, then intent flow); part of Principle 6 (Step 0b)"
    
    - id: "2.4"
      title: "IVD in Action: The CSV Example Revisited"
      required_content:
        - "Recall Ch1 CSV mismatch (you prompt, AI builds wrong, many turns)"
        - "With IVD: AI writes intent artifact, you review, AI implements, AI verifies"
        - "Inline YAML example showing structured intent with constraints and tests"
        - "Key outcome: turns drop from many to one; hallucinations caught by tests"
        - "Quotable: **From 'Hope It Works' to 'Prove It Works'**"
    
    - id: "2.5"
      title: "The Eight Principles: An Overview"
      required_content:
        - "List all 8 principles with one-line summary each (not deep dive—Ch3 does that)"
        - "Explain that principles work as a system, not independent rules"
        - "Hook to Chapter 3: 'The next chapter explores each principle in depth'"
    
    - id: "2.6"
      title: "Why This Works: Executable Understanding Fails Loudly"
      required_content:
        - "Prose can be wrong silently; executable constraints fail loudly"
        - "The AI runs tests after implementing—catches its own hallucinations"
        - "Quotable: **Prose fails silently. Executable understanding fails loudly.**"
        - "Summary: IVD is the framework for AI Agents era—AI writes, implements, verifies"
        - "Bridge to Ch3: understan

- "Explain that principles work as a system, not independent rules"
        - "Hook to Chapter 3: 'The next chapter explores each principle in depth'"
    
    - id: "2.6"
      title: "Why This Works: Executable Understanding Fails Loudly"
      required_content:
        - "Prose can be wrong silently; executable constraints fail loudly"
        - "The AI runs tests after implementing—catches its own hallucinations"
        - "Quotable: **Prose fails silently. Executable understanding fails loudly.**"
        - "Summary: IVD is the framework for AI Agents era—AI writes, implements, verifies"
        - "Bridge to Ch3: understand the principles deeply before building"
  
  manuscript:
    file: "chapter.md"
    required_elements:
      - "Key Points or Summary block at end (constraint: chapter_takeaways)"
      - "1–2 quotable, tweetable lines (constraint: quotable_principles)"
      - "One inline intent artifact example (YAML code block)"
      - "Clear bridge from Ch1 problem to Ch2 solution"

# ==============================================================================
# KEY POINTS (from book_system_intent; must be reflected in chapter)
# ==============================================================================

key_points:
  - "IVD is the framework for the AI Agents era—solves many-turns and hallucinations"
  - "The AI writes the intent artifact (not the human), then implements and verifies"
  - "The eight principles of IVD (overview; deep dive in Ch3)"
  - "Why this works: executable understanding fails loudly—hallucinations are caught at the source"

# ==============================================================================
# CONSTRAINTS (from book intent; chapter-specific)
# ==============================================================================

constraints:
  - name: "chapter_takeaways"
    requirement: "Chapter ends with 'Key Points' or 'Summary' block"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.chapter_takeaways"
    validation: "chapter.md contains a ## Key Points or ## Summary section"
  
  - name: "quotable_principles"
    requirement: "1–2 memorable, tweetable lines"
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.quotable_lines"
    validation: "Manual: at least one line is quotable on its own"
    expected_quotables:
      - "Intent is Primary. Code and documentation are derived."
      - "From 'Hope It Works' to 'Prove It Works'"
      - "Prose fails silently. Executable understanding fails loudly."
  
  - name: "writing_style_part_1"
    requirement: "Prose leads; examples illustrate. Mentor-like, direct, second person. High depth, high accessibility."
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style (voice, tone, code_and_prose.part_1_3_4)"
    validation: "Manual: voice and tone match"
  
  - name: "formatting_conventions"
    requirement: "Follow book_system_intent.writing_style.formatting_conventions: bold for key terms and Key Points, italic for emphasis and dialogue, code formatting for technical terms, proper paragraph structure (3-6 sentences typical, 1-2 for emphasis, 7-10 for dense exposition, >10 MUST be broken with subheadings or split)."
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.formatt

illustrate. Mentor-like, direct, second person. High depth, high accessibility."
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style (voice, tone, code_and_prose.part_1_3_4)"
    validation: "Manual: voice and tone match"
  
  - name: "formatting_conventions"
    requirement: "Follow book_system_intent.writing_style.formatting_conventions: bold for key terms and Key Points, italic for emphasis and dialogue, code formatting for technical terms, proper paragraph structure (3-6 sentences typical, 1-2 for emphasis, 7-10 for dense exposition, >10 MUST be broken with subheadings or split)."
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.formatting_conventions"
    validation: "Manual: formatting matches conventions (bold/italic/code usage, NO paragraphs >10 sentences without subheadings)"
  
  - name: "concrete_example"
    requirement: "Include at least one concrete IVD example (CSV scenario revisited) with inline intent artifact YAML"
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.clarity_tactics (examples); research/best_selling_programming_books.md 6.1 (author-sourced examples)"
    validation: "Section 2.4 contains YAML code block showing intent artifact"
  
  - name: "author_sourced_examples"
    requirement: "The CSV example (2.4) must derive from the author's direct experience (anonymized) or be explicitly labeled as composite/illustrative. Prefer author's experience."
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.author_sourced_examples; research/best_selling_programming_books.md 6.1"
    validation: "2.4 is from author's experience (anon.) or has explicit 'composite'/'illustrative' label; no unanchored fiction"
  
  - name: "principles_overview_not_deep_dive"
    requirement: "Section 2.5 lists all 8 principles with one-line summaries only. Deep explanation is in Chapter 3."
    source: "book_system_intent.book_structure (Ch2 overview, Ch3 depth)"
    validation: "2.5 has 8 principles; each has ≤2 sentences. No multi-paragraph explanations."
  
  - name: "no_unsourced_industry_claims"
    requirement: "Do not claim specific companies (GitHub, Anthropic, OpenAI) use IVD without sources. Use general 'industry patterns' or 'similar approaches' language if needed."
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.evidence_based"
    validation: "No unsourced claims about specific companies adopting IVD"

# ==============================================================================
# RATIONALE
# ==============================================================================

rationale:
  decision: "Chapter 2 introduces IVD paradigm; Chapter 3 goes deep on principles."
  
  reasoning: |
    - Ch1 establishes the problem; Ch2 must answer the question "What if intent was verifiable?"
    - Pattern from bestsellers: problem (Ch1) → paradigm/solution overview (Ch2) → principles in depth (Ch3)
    - Concrete example (2.4) bridges abstract to concrete—reader sees IVD in action before principle details
    - 8 principles listed but not deep-dived: prevents Ch2 bloat; creates desire for Ch3
    - Removed unsourced "GitHub, Anthropic, OpenAI" claim—no evidence; replaced with concrete CSV example
  
  evidence: "book/book_system_intent.yaml; ivd_system_intent.yaml (

digm; Chapter 3 goes deep on principles."
  
  reasoning: |
    - Ch1 establishes the problem; Ch2 must answer the question "What if intent was verifiable?"
    - Pattern from bestsellers: problem (Ch1) → paradigm/solution overview (Ch2) → principles in depth (Ch3)
    - Concrete example (2.4) bridges abstract to concrete—reader sees IVD in action before principle details
    - 8 principles listed but not deep-dived: prevents Ch2 bloat; creates desire for Ch3
    - Removed unsourced "GitHub, Anthropic, OpenAI" claim—no evidence; replaced with concrete CSV example
  
  evidence: "book/book_system_intent.yaml; ivd_system_intent.yaml (8 principles); research/best_selling_programming_books.md (structure, examples)"
  date: "2026-01-26"
  stakeholder: "IVD book author"

# ==============================================================================
# RISKS
# ==============================================================================

risks:
  - condition: "Chapter oversells IVD without sufficient concrete evidence"
    action: "Keep claims grounded in the CSV example (2.4); avoid sweeping industry claims without sources. Part III provides the heavy evidence."
    monitor: "Beta reader feedback: 'did you believe it?'"
    severity: "medium"
  
  - condition: "Principles overview (2.5) becomes a deep dive, bloating Ch2 and stealing from Ch3"
    action: "Enforce ≤2 sentences per principle in 2.5; deep explanations belong in Ch3"
    monitor: "Word count of section 2.5 relative to other sections"
    severity: "low"
  
  - condition: "Reader confuses 'AI writes intent' with 'AI decides what to build'"
    action: "Section 2.3 must clearly state: human describes what they want, AI structures it as intent, human reviews. The human retains decision authority."
    monitor: "Beta reader comprehension test"
    severity: "medium"

# ==============================================================================
# IMPLEMENTATION
# ==============================================================================

implementation:
  intent_artifact: "manuscript/part-1-foundations/chapter-02-ivd-paradigm/chapter_02_ivd_paradigm_intent.yaml"
  output: "manuscript/part-1-foundations/chapter-02-ivd-paradigm/chapter.md"
  status: "complete"
  version: 1
  
  process:
    reference: "book/chapter_writing_process.yaml"
    current_step: "step_7_complete"
    completed_steps:
      - "step_1_verify_prerequisites"
      - "step_2_create_chapter_folder"
      - "step_3_create_chapter_intent"
      - "step_4_write_manuscript"
      - "step_5_cross_check"
      - "step_6_update_chapter_status"
      - "step_7_update_book_intent"

# ==============================================================================
# VERIFICATION (cross-check with book intent)
# ==============================================================================

verification:
  cross_check:
    - check: "All key_points from book_system_intent Ch2 appear or are clearly implied in chapter"
      validates: "intent.success_metric"
    - check: "All deliverables.sections (2.1–2.6) are present with required_content"
      validates: "deliverables"
    - check: "Manuscript has Key Points/Summary and 1–2

cross_check"
      - "step_6_update_chapter_status"
      - "step_7_update_book_intent"

# ==============================================================================
# VERIFICATION (cross-check with book intent)
# ==============================================================================

verification:
  cross_check:
    - check: "All key_points from book_system_intent Ch2 appear or are clearly implied in chapter"
      validates: "intent.success_metric"
    - check: "All deliverables.sections (2.1–2.6) are present with required_content"
      validates: "deliverables"
    - check: "Manuscript has Key Points/Summary and 1–2 quotable lines"
      validates: "constraints.chapter_takeaways, quotable_principles"
    - check: "Section 2.4 has inline YAML intent artifact example"
      validates: "constraints.concrete_example"
    - check: "Section 2.5 lists all 8 principles with one-line summaries (not deep dive)"
      validates: "constraints.principles_overview_not_deep_dive"
    - check: "No unsourced claims about specific companies using IVD"
      validates: "constraints.no_unsourced_industry_claims"
    - check: "Formatting follows conventions (bold/italic/code usage)"
      validates: "constraints.formatting_conventions"

# ==============================================================================
# CHANGELOG
# ==============================================================================

changelog:
  - version: "1.3"
    date: "2026-02-09"
    change: |
      Full manuscript-vs-intent cross-check. Findings and fixes:
      MANUSCRIPT:
      (1) `principles_overview_not_deep_dive` constraint violation: P1 (3 sentences + parenthetical),
          P5 (3 sentences), and P8 (3 sentences with Ch3-level "Use it when..." guidance) exceeded
          the ≤2 sentences per principle limit. Trimmed: P1 combined into 1 sentence; P5 merged 2nd
          and 3rd sentences; P8 removed "Use it when..." clause (belongs in Ch3).
      (2) `author_sourced_examples` constraint gap: CSV example in 2.4 not labeled as author-sourced
          or illustrative. Added "industry-typical" label (matching Ch1's framing).
      No other issues found — all key_points, deliverables, constraints, formatting conventions,
      and risk checks passed.
    reason: "Manuscript cross-check: fix minor constraint violations in principles overview and example labeling."
  - version: "1.2"
    date: "2026-02-09"
    change: |
      Cross-check against book_system_intent v1.6, research, outline, and template:
      (1) Header Version "1.0" → "1.2", Status "Pending" → "Complete" (manuscript exists, implementation.status was already complete).
      (2) Added parent_intent: "../../../book_system_intent.yaml" (template requirement for non-system intents).
      (3) Synced master intent Ch2 key_points to AI-era reframe ("AI writes the intent", "many-turns and hallucinations")
          and section titles (2.3 "The AI Writes and Verifies", 2.4 dropped "PM/Dev"). Master was stale.
      (4) Added author_sourced_examples constraint for section 2.4 CSV example (missing; Ch1 had it).
      (5) Fixed stale "PM/dev CSV" in concrete_example constraint → "CSV scenario" (per v1.1 ref

      (2) Added parent_intent: "../../../book_system_intent.yaml" (template requirement for non-system intents).
      (3) Synced master intent Ch2 key_points to AI-era reframe ("AI writes the intent", "many-turns and hallucinations")
          and section titles (2.3 "The AI Writes and Verifies", 2.4 dropped "PM/Dev"). Master was stale.
      (4) Added author_sourced_examples constraint for section 2.4 CSV example (missing; Ch1 had it).
      (5) Fixed stale "PM/dev CSV" in concrete_example constraint → "CSV scenario" (per v1.1 reframe).
      (6) Added risks section with 3 risks: overselling, principles bloat, AI-decides confusion.
    reason: "Alignment audit: chapter intent must match master intent, research, outline, and template conventions."
  - version: "1.1"
    date: "2026-01-26"
    change: "Major reframe: IVD is for the AI Agents era. Key insight: the AI writes the intent (not the human). Updated intent, goal, key_points, and all deliverables to focus on: AI writes intent, AI implements, AI verifies. Removed 'PM/dev' framing."
    reason: "IVD is NOT for traditional PM > Developer workflows. IVD is the framework for AI agents. The novelty is: AI writes intent following IVD, reducing turns and catching hallucinations."
  - version: "1.0"
    date: "2026-01-26"
    change: "Initial chapter intent for Chapter 2 'Intent-Verified Development: A New Paradigm'"
    reason: "IVD: intent before implementation. Removed unsourced industry validation section; added concrete CSV example (2.4). Clarified Ch2 overview vs Ch3 depth."
